description: Some relationships are missing from this knowledge graph. Read the text, explore the graph, and add the missing triples.
text:
NovaTech is an AI company founded in 2015, headquartered in San Francisco. The company's flagship product is NovaDB, a high-performance database. Alice Chen serves as CEO, while Bob Rao is the CTO. Elena Ruiz works at NovaTech as Lead Scientist.

DataFlow specializes in data analytics and was founded by David Kim in 2018. The company is headquartered in Austin and develops FlowEngine, an ETL pipeline tool. Carol Zhang serves as VP of Engineering at DataFlow.

CloudPeak, a cloud infrastructure provider founded in 2012, is headquartered in San Francisco. Their main product is PeakCloud. CloudPeak made an early investment in NovaTech, and the two companies maintain a strategic partnership.

QuantumLeap is a quantum computing startup founded in 2020, headquartered in Seattle. Frank Liu is the CEO and Grace Patel serves as CTO. Their product QuantumSDK is a development kit for quantum applications. QuantumLeap invested in BioSynth, and CloudPeak and QuantumLeap have a strategic partnership.

BioSynth is a biotech company founded in 2016, headquartered in Austin. Henry Okafor is the Research Director and leads development of BioAnalyzer, their flagship analysis tool.

In a major industry move, NovaTech acquired DataFlow, bringing DataFlow's analytics capabilities in-house.

Alice Chen, who was born in San Francisco, attended Stanford University before building her career in tech. Bob Rao is an MIT graduate. David Kim also attended Stanford University before founding DataFlow. Frank Liu, born in Seattle, attended UC Berkeley. Grace Patel graduated from MIT. Henry Okafor studied at UC Berkeley. Elena Ruiz was born in Austin.

text:
NovaTech is an AI company founded in 2015, headquartered in San Francisco. The company's flagship product is NovaDB, a high-performance database. Alice Chen serves as CEO, while Bob Rao is the CTO. Elena Ruiz works at NovaTech as Lead Scientist.

DataFlow specializes in data analytics and was founded by David Kim in 2018. The company is headquartered in Austin and develops FlowEngine, an ETL pipeline tool. Carol Zhang serves as VP of Engineering at DataFlow.

CloudPeak, a cloud infrastructure provider founded in 2012, is headquartered in San Francisco. Their main product is PeakCloud. CloudPeak made an early investment in NovaTech, and the two companies maintain a strategic partnership.

QuantumLeap is a quantum computing startup founded in 2020, headquartered in Seattle. Frank Liu is the CEO and Grace Patel serves as CTO. Their product QuantumSDK is a development kit for quantum applications. QuantumLeap invested in BioSynth, and CloudPeak and QuantumLeap have a strategic partnership.

BioSynth is a biotech company founded in 2016, headquartered in Austin. Henry Okafor is the Research Director and leads development of BioAnalyzer, their flagship analysis tool.

In a major industry move, NovaTech acquired DataFlow, bringing DataFlow's analytics capabilities in-house.

Alice Chen, who was born in San Francisco, attended Stanford University before building her career in tech. Bob Rao is an MIT graduate. David Kim also attended Stanford University before founding DataFlow. Frank Liu, born in Seattle, attended UC Berkeley. Grace Patel graduated from MIT. Henry Okafor studied at UC Berkeley. Elena Ruiz was born in Austin.
